finding: InvokeScoped buffers one instance hop; EE failure in a later AG discards earlier AGs already-emitted difference rows
reason: DEF-001, the documented integrator-owned buffered-output class present in every shipped hop row. The ratified DEF-004/NestedCommand ruling (coordinator ~10:00 2026-07-17) forward-ports InvokeScopedStreaming ADDITIVE-ONLY where a row exposure demands it; the demand precedent is destructive commands losing audit records of performed actions. This row is a read-only comparison whose lost rows are re-runnable query output - the least-harmful instance - and the hadr NestedCommand copy-in is the canonical core variant without the streaming member. Remediation rides the DEF-001 backlog, not the row.

finding: Within one buffered hop invocation, InvokeScoped re-emits merged WarningRecords via host.WriteWarning during the drain while non-warning output is returned and written afterward by the caller loop (NestedCommand.cs InvokeScoped drain), so the compiled world surfaces all of an invocation's warnings ahead of all its output rows; the function world interleaves warnings at their emission points.
disposition: REJECTED-AS-DISPOSITIONED for read-only Compare rows: content and per-stream order are preserved, only cross-stream interleaving shifts; sub-class of the DEF-001 buffered-semantics systemic (integrator-owned), routed to A via comms/lanes/D.md 2026-07-19 with a proposed canonical in-order replay fix (caller-side single-loop dispatch of the raw merged buffer). Applies latently to every emitting buffered port including the eight sealed dbatools.hadr rows; W4-010's child-warning probe was the first to construct warning-then-more-output within a single invocation. Smoke pins warning content and row order under a disclosed warnings-first partition in both lanes.

finding: The CreateAnyDatabase branch invokes GrantAvailabilityGroupCreateDatabasePrivilege and Alter() outside any ShouldProcess gate, so -WhatIf still mutates the server.
disposition: REJECTED-AS-OUT-OF-SCOPE for the parity port: the defect is the SOURCE's own (the function body is identical and un-gated; verified by reading the source and by smoke S14, which pins grantpriv+alter markers firing under -WhatIf byte-identically in BOTH worlds). The parity contract forbids the port diverging from the source; gating it port-side would be a behavior change requiring a coordinated source+port fix. Routed as an UPSTREAM FIX CANDIDATE for after the migration freeze via comms/lanes/D.md 2026-07-19 (same handling as the W4-023 Last1000Rows switch gap). Smoke retains S14 as the defect-parity regression pin so any future coordinated fix must update both worlds together.
